content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

February 27, 1990

Bev Langston
18 Oakcrest Lane
Newport Beach, CA 92660

Re: Your Request For Advice
Our File No. A-90-041

Dear Ms. Langston:

You have requested advice concerning the campaign provisions
of the Political Reform Act.1/

QUESTION

Are certain expenses associated with a weekend fishing trip
provided to persons who make contributions to Senator Marian
Bergerson considered non-monetary contributions to her campaign?

CONCLUSION

A fundraising event held in an individual's home is not a
contribution if the total cost of the event is $500 or less.
If the cost of the meals, air transportation, and entertainment
provided to participants amounts to more than $500, all payments
for these items will constitute non-monetary contributions to
Senator Bergeson.

FACTS

To help raise campaign funds for Marian Bergeson for Lt.
Governor, you plan to provide a weekend fishing trip to
individuals who contribute $1,000 to Senator Bergeson's campaign
committee. Three of the events have been planned. Lodging will
be provided in a private residence owned by five partners,
including you and four other families. At least one of the
owners will be present. Private air transportation may
be provided by various partners of the residence. Use of boats
and fishing equipment will also be available.

_________________________
1/ Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory
references are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated.
Commission regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations
Section 18000, et seq. All references to regulations are to Title
2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.

428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916) 322-5660

Bev Langston
Page 2


ANALYSIS

The term "contribution" does not include:

...a payment made by an occupant of a home or office for
costs related to any meeting or fundraising event held in
the occupant's home or office if the costs for the meeting
or fundraising event are five hundred dollars ($500) or less.

Section 82015.

If the cost of the meals, air transportation, entertainment
and out of pocket expenses, including any additional equipment or
supplies purchased (e.g., fishing equipment, bait, etc.) amounts
to more than $500, all payments for these items must be reported
by Senator Bergeson as a non-monetary contribution. This includes
air transportation or other items provided by others in connection
with the fishing trip.

To determine the value of the air transportation, you should
estimate the fair market value by using the commercial air rate,
or, alternatively, the charter rate divided by the number of
passengers. (In re Stone (1977) 3 FPPC Ops. 52, copy enclosed.)

The Act places limitations on contributions to candidates for
elective office based on a fiscal year (July 1 - June 30).
(Sections 85301-85303.) Monetary and non-monetary contributions,
as well as loans and enforceable promises, from a "person" to a
candidate are limited to $1,000 per fiscal year. (Section 85301.)
A "person" includes an individual, corporation, or other entity.
(Section 85102(b).)

If you have additional questions, please contact me at (916)
322-5662.

Sincerely,

Kathryn E. Donovan
General Counsel

By: Alice Hughes
Political Reform Consultant

Enclosures
